recommendations.title: Fences
recommendations.overview: In 1950s Pittsburgh, a frustrated African-American father struggles with the constraints of poverty, racism, and his own inner demons as he tries to raise a family.

recommendations.title: The Great Debaters
recommendations.overview: The true story of a brilliant but politically radical debate team coach who uses the power of words to transform a group of underdog African-American college students into a historical powerhouse that took on the Harvard elite.

recommendations.title: Antwone Fisher
recommendations.overview: A sailor prone to violent outbursts is sent to a naval psychiatrist for help. Refusing at first to open up, the young man eventually breaks down and reveals a horrific childhood. Through the guidance of his doctor, he confronts his painful past and b

recommendations.title: Glory
recommendations.overview: Robert Gould Shaw leads the US Civil War's first all-black volunteer company, fighting prejudices of both his own Union army and the Confederates.

recommendations.title: John Q
recommendations.overview: John Quincy Archibald is a father and husband whose son is diagnosed with an enlarged heart and then finds out he cannot receive a transplant because HMO insurance will not cover it. Therefore, he decides to take a hospital full of patients hostage u

recommendations.title: Only You
recommendations.overview: A madly-in-love young couple's relationship begins to suffer when they struggle to conceive a child.

recommendations.title: Chemical Hearts
recommendations.overview: When a hopelessly romantic high school senior falls for a mysterious new classmate, it sets them both on an unexpected journey that teaches them about love, loss, and most importantly themselves.

recommendations.title: The King and the Mockingbird
recommendations.overview: A young shepherdess and a chimneysweep plan to get married and escape the clutches of a tyrannical king in love with her, assisted by the guile of a cheeky mockingbird, the king's archenemy.

recommendations.title: Queen Margot
recommendations.overview: Paris, Kingdom of France, August 18, 1572. To avoid the outbreak of a religious war, the Catholic princess Marguerite de Valois, sister of the feeble King Charles IX, marries the Huguenot King Henry III of Navarre.

recommendations.title: A Little Thing Called Love
recommendations.overview: An introverted young girl falls in love with a popular senior. However, as she begins to come out of her shell, she faces several new problems.
recommendations.director: Wasin Pokpong, Puttipong Promsaka Na Sakolnakorn
